recap.title: Session Recap
recap.intro: Check the facts you want in the draft, then draft the recap.
recap.empty: Nothing notable was logged this sitting — you can still draft a blank recap.

warband.title: Player mice
warband.empty: No mice yet — add your first player character.
warband.deleteMessage: This removes {name} from the roster. This can't be undone — but nothing else in the campaign changes.

hirelings.title: Hired help
hirelings.empty: No hirelings yet — add one when the warband hires help.
hirelings.deleteMessage: This removes {name} from the roster. This can't be undone — but nothing else in the campaign changes.
hirelings.hirelingLimitWarning: This party has more hirelings than its mice can command (WIL limit) — still fine to play, but Loyalty checks may get messy.

inventory.heading: Inventory
inventory.overburdenedMessage: A mouse can't carry more than 10 slots — drop something before the next scene.

adventure.empty: No beats yet — outline your first plot thread.
adventure.deleteMessage: This removes "{title}" from the outline. This can't be undone — but nothing else in the campaign changes.
adventure.deleteMessageWithChildren: This removes "{title}" and its {count} sub-beats from the outline. This can't be undone — but nothing else in the campaign changes.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.empty: No sessions logged yet — log one right after you play.
sessions.deleteMessage: This removes "{title}" from the session log. This can't be undone — but nothing else in the campaign changes.

form.summaryPlaceholder: What happened, what changed, what's next…
form.autoGenerated: Auto-generated draft — edit or delete freely

bestiary.empty: No creatures yet — add your first stat block.

factions.title: Factions
factions.empty: No factions yet — add your first power in the meadow.
factions.deleteMessage: This removes {name} and its relationships. This can't be undone — but nothing else in the campaign changes.

graph.heading: Relationships
graph.empty: Add factions to see the relationship web.

form.name: Name
form.relationshipsAddFirst: Save the faction first, then add relationships.

hexMap.empty: No hexes charted yet — tap a hex to describe it.
hexMap.deleteMessage: This clears {name} from the map. This can't be undone — but nothing else in the campaign changes.

form.name: Name
form.encountersNoBestiary: Add entries to the Bestiary first to link encounters here.

encounter.title: Random encounter
encounter.noBestiary: No bestiary entries yet — add creatures in the Bestiary screen.

notice.title: Your campaign lives only in this browser.
notice.body: There's no account and no server — everything you enter is saved locally on this device. Clearing browser data, using private or incognito mode, or switching devices or browsers will lose it. Export your campaign regularly (below) to keep a backup.

language.title: Language
language.hint: Changes labels and text throughout the app immediately.

data.title: Campaign data
data.intro: Export everything — parties, hirelings, adventures, bestiary, factions, hex map, and session logs — into one file you can back up or move to another device.
data.exportHint: Download a single JSON file with your full campaign.
data.importHint: Restore from a previously exported file. This replaces everything currently in the app.
data.resetHint: Clear all campaign data and start fresh. This can't be undone — export first if you want to keep your work.
data.footer: Tip: export after every session so nothing is lost if this browser's storage is cleared.
data.confirmMessage: Importing will overwrite everything currently saved in this browser. This can't be undone unless you have another export.
data.resetConfirmMessage: This will permanently delete all your campaign data (parties, adventures, factions, etc.) and can't be undone. Make sure you've exported everything you want to keep.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.empty: Nothing recorded yet — this fills in as you complete beats, log sessions, and bump faction clocks.

guidance.hostile: Attacks or otherwise acts against the party.
guidance.unfriendly: Does the opposite of what's asked, may attack.
guidance.neutral: Uncertain, will act to preserve itself.
guidance.friendly: Does as asked, if not put in danger.
guidance.helpful: Actively helps, and offers a service.

saves.heading: Saves
saves.body: Roll 1d20 and succeed on a result at or under the relevant score (STR/DEX/WIL — the GM picks based on the fiction). Rolling exactly the score is a pass. Plain pass/fail — nothing special happens on a natural 1 or 20.
saves.note: SRD note: this app does not use crit-success/fumble on saves.

damage.heading: Damage & the death spiral
damage.body: Damage hits HP first. Once HP is empty, remaining damage overflows into STR. Any STR lost means an immediate STR save is required. A failed STR save applies Injured and Incapacitated. If STR is driven to exactly 0, the mouse dies immediately — no save, no wound roll.
damage.note: SRD note: this app skips the SRD's 2d6 Fatal Wounds table — 0 STR is a clean death, not a roll for what kind of wound it is.

conditions.heading: Conditions
conditions.intro: Mausritter's fixed condition vocabulary — every condition a mouse or hireling carries is one of these six:

exhausted.label: Exhausted
exhausted.note: Disadvantage on STR and DEX saves. Cleared by a full rest.

frightened.label: Frightened
frightened.note: Must pass a WIL save to approach the source of the fear. Cleared by a short rest away from danger.

hungryThirsty.label: Hungry & Thirsty
hungryThirsty.note: Save penalty until fed and watered. Cleared by eating a proper meal.

injured.label: Injured
injured.note: Disadvantage on STR and DEX saves. Cleared by a full rest.

incapacitated.label: Incapacitated
incapacitated.note: Out of the fight — from a failed STR save. Cleared once tended or stabilized, not by resting alone.

unconscious.label: Unconscious
unconscious.note: Out cold from a Fatal Wound. Cleared by tending or rest.

inventory.heading: Inventory & slots
inventory.body: Every mouse and hireling has a fixed 10 slots: 6 body + 4 paws (paws fill first, for quick access). Most items take 1 slot; bulky gear (heavy armor, two-handed weapons, large treasure) takes 2. A handful of items (worn clothes, natural claws and teeth) take 0. Chargeable items (lanterns, spells, rope) track charges on a wear pip-track — tick one down per use. Going over 10 slots shows a warning, not a hard block.

reaction.heading: Reaction roll
reaction.intro: Flat 2d6, no score to roll under — just read the band off the total:

advancement.heading: Advancement
advancement.body: XP comes from pips spent in downtime (carousing, gear, keepsakes) — never from kills or combat.
advancement.thresholds: Level thresholds: 1,000 / 3,000 / 6,000 / 11,000 / 16,000 XP, then +5,000 XP per level after that.

retainers.heading: Retainer limit
retainers.body: A mouse can command a number of hirelings equal to their WIL score. This is per mouse, summed across the whole party for the party's total hireling capacity.
